segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with an ESRB 'TEEN' rating screen, detailing content descriptors like Mild Violence, Language, In-Game Purchases (including Random Items), and User Interaction. An intense, building electronic score begins as the scene transitions to a dark, industrial hangar where a metallic hexagonal logo with a stylized 'T' emerges, glowing orange. The bold title 'WORLD OF TANKS 2.0' appears in red and white, accompanied by a male voice declaring, "World of Tanks 2.0 is the biggest and most important update in the game's history. It marks the start of a new era." The music intensifies with tank engine sounds, gunfire, and explosions as dynamic gameplay footage unfolds: a tank fires from rocky terrain, moves through a lush forest with a river, and is seen on a bridge overlooking a cityscape. A massive formation of tanks is shown on a grassy field under various national flags, while on-screen text highlights new features: 'NEW MAP AND STORY MISSION', 'RANDOM EVENTS', 'MATCHMAKING', 'NEW SOUNDS', and 'THIRD PERSONAL MISSIONS CAMPAIGN'. The scene shifts to a high-tech garage with a spotlight on a tank, then displays a '2.0 INTERFACE AND GARAGE' with player stats and mission objectives. The male voice returns, stating, "Let's begin with the all-new garage." The camera pans through the expansive, detailed garage, revealing multiple tanks, a motorcycle, and industrial equipment, as the voice describes it: "The new garage reflects the scale of the changes. It's spacious and richly..." The audio cuts off as the final frames focus on a close-up of a vintage motorcycle in a dimly lit workshop, surrounded by tools and numbered crates.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a sweeping view of the New Garage Environment, a spacious, modern industrial hangar with high ceilings, exposed metal beams, and large windows, where a male narrator describes it as "detailed, with contrast lighting, new music, and surround sound," creating a "spirit of preparation for intense battles." He explains this is "where vehicles are designed, assembled, painted, and repaired," with "all areas united in a single complex, creating the atmosphere of a real tank factory." Spotlights cast dramatic beams across the hazy floor, illuminating various military vehicles, including tanks and a Vintage Motorcycle, alongside industrial equipment. The camera pans across a camouflaged tank before focusing on a tan-colored wheeled 'Concept 5' tank positioned centrally. The scene transitions to the Game Interface Screen, displaying '2.0 INTERFACE AND GARAGE', where the narrator announces, "The garage interface has also been updated." Player stats (7,567 credits, 3,083,659 experience, 175,943 gold) and a 'BATTLE!' button are visible, while the narrator details how "The top of the screen has become clearer, as the game menu list has been moved to the left and stacked vertically." He adds that "The vehicle's crew has been moved next to equipment, consumables, and other vehicle loadout menus, so all pre-battle preparation now happens in one place," and that "The vehicle panel can now be expanded by pressing the spacebar." The player navigates the 'My Vehicles' menu, showing tanks organized by nation, then creates a new playlist by dragging the 'Jg.Pz. E 100' tank into the 'New Playlist 1' area, all set against the consistent industrial hangar backdrop, accompanied by a dramatic, industrial background score.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a user in the 'New Playlist Interface', creating a 'Kaboom' playlist by dragging tanks like the Jg.Pz. E 100, KV-2, and FV4005. A male narrator, accompanied by upbeat electronic music, explains how users can "quickly scroll through all your vehicles" and "create your own custom vehicle lists, just like music playlists for different moods." The interface then transitions to the 'My Vehicles Menu', displaying a grid of tanks organized by nation, where the user navigates through playlists such as 'Low Tiers', 'Platoon', and 'Kaboom'. The narrator adds that "the familiar filters still work as before" and that "the right side of the screen contains all the latest events, in-game activities, Battle Pass, daily missions, all in one place." The user selects the tan-colored 'TVP VTU' tank, which appears in the spacious 'New Garage Environment'. The narrator highlights that "all vehicle information is now displayed in a new window," allowing users to find "a description of its mechanics, all its technical parameters, the module research tab, and its armor layout" in just a few clicks. The user then switches to the 'Armor' tab, viewing a 3D model of the tank with color-coded armor layers, and compares it with the 'E 100', noting its 280mm turret armor and 395mm effective armor at 45°. The narrator emphasizes that "the new armor tab lets you learn how to block damage effectively and where to aim to penetrate your opponent's armor," providing "a detailed breakdown of the vehicle's armor." The scene remains within the modern garage, focusing on the tank's design and specifications.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a detailed 3D armor inspection of the E 100, a German heavy tank, displayed in a dimly lit industrial hangar. A male narrator explains how to "hover the cursor over any point to see the exact effective armor thickness at the selected angle," as color-coded armor layers and stats like 280mm turret armor and 395mm effective armor at 45° are shown. The scene transitions to a victory screen, which the narrator identifies as a "comprehensive post-battle statistics screen," displaying battle results. The view then shifts to the TVP VTU Concept tank in the garage, highlighting its stats and armor, followed by the main garage screen showcasing the Concept 5 tank with a 'BATTLE!' button and player resources. The narrator emphasizes that "the new interface and garage are an important part of World of Tanks 2.0," making the interface "more functional and better organized." Dramatic, futuristic music builds as the video cuts to a cinematic sequence featuring a massive 'X' structure in a snowy, stormy landscape, accompanied by lightning and sound effects. The narrator excitedly announces "Tier 11" and "16 brand new vehicles at once," describing it as "a major leap forward" and "an all-new picture of battle." This is visually reinforced by a tank firing in a rocky terrain with explosions and smoke, and later a tank moving through a grassy field, firing its cannon. The scene changes to a tech tree interface displaying German tanks, including the E 100 and Maus, with tier progression and cost details, as the narrator instructs viewers to "research it for XP and purchase it" to acquire a Tier 11 vehicle, concluding with a dynamic shot of a tank moving through a grassy field before returning to the tech tree, emphasizing the progression path to Tier XI tanks.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with an epic, action-oriented soundtrack as a detailed view of the German tech tree in a game interface is shown, highlighting Tier XI tanks like the E 100 and Maus, with player resources visible. A narrator begins, stating, "Seven heavy tanks, five medium tanks, three tank destroyers, and one light tank await," explaining that "All these vehicles continue their existing tech tree branches with their own diverse play styles." The scene transitions to a cinematic showcase of a dark-colored Tier XI tank with a long barrel, moving through rocky, mountainous terrain under a bright sky, firing its cannon with a powerful blast. The narrator emphasizes, "Each Tier 11 vehicle has its own unique mechanic," which could be "an active ability that you can use at the right moment," "a passive effect that works automatically," or "a situational bonus that activates in certain conditions." The camera then pans across a vast tank formation field, where hundreds of tanks, marked by national flags, are arranged in neat rows against a castle and mountains. The action shifts to a war-torn urban environment, filled with explosions and tank engine sounds, where a tank maneuvers through rubble and fires at an enemy tank, the T603, causing a large, fiery explosion. The narrator continues, noting that some mechanics "boost firepower" while others "enhance mobility," as the scene changes to a grassy, hilly landscape where another tank engages an enemy, the Cameron AX, firing its cannon with a loud report and causing another fiery explosion. The tank advances on a dirt road, engaging multiple enemies with damage indicators and critical hits appearing on screen amidst continuous firing and explosions. The final scenes show the tank in a forested area, moving through tall grass with engine sounds, firing at an enemy, with smoke and debris flying as the enemy tank is destroyed, capturing the dynamic combat and diverse environments of the game, emphasizing the power and precision of Tier XI tanks.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with the camouflaged AMX 67 Imbattable, a Tier XI French Support Heavy Tank, engaging in dynamic combat within a grassy, forested landscape, its cannon firing with a powerful boom as intense, dramatic orchestral music plays. A first-person scope view shows targeting an enemy at 304 meters, while a calm male voice explains that "all of them highlight each vehicle's unique playstyle." The scene transitions to the game's modern, well-lit garage, where the AMX 67 is displayed. UI clicks accompany the player navigating the upgrade tree, researching nodes like 'Reload Time - Firepower' and 'Engine Power - Mobility'. The voiceover details this "upgraded system exclusive to Tier 11, replacing field modifications," which makes "each of the 16 vehicles even more effective by improving both their base characteristics and special mechanics." After fully researching the tree, the elite rewards screen appears, showcasing a 'Destroyed Vehicles' stat tracker, a 'Lignes de Force' volumetric style, and a 'Beast' gun sleeve 3D attachment. The voice confirms these are "customization elements unique to Tier 11," specifically mentioning "a stat tracker, volumetric 2D styles and gun sleeves." The video concludes with cinematic shots of the AMX 67 in a rocky, industrial battlefield and a grassy field featuring a large 'XI' symbol, accompanied by more tank firing and explosion sounds, as the voice encourages viewers to "play at the new tier," reinforcing its Tier XI status and the '2.0 THE BIG REBALANCE' update theme, all set to the continuing dramatic music.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a sweeping aerial view of the Tank Formation Field, where hundreds of tanks are arranged in precise rows under national flags, set against mountains and a distant castle, as a male narrator introduces 'Update 2.0' and 'the big rebalance' as crucial for improving gameplay, set to upbeat, slightly dramatic music. The scene transitions to a dark, digital game interface displaying 'THE BIG REBALANCE' with tank profiles like the E 50 and Panhard EBR 90, as the narrator details the 'broad and systematic' rebalance, including Tier 11 introduction and changes across the 'entire vehicle roster.' A dynamic combat sequence then unfolds on a tropical beach, showing the AMX 67 Imbattable firing its cannon, splashing through water, and maneuvering around rocky terrain, as the narrator emphasizes that these are 'complex adjustments' designed to 'enhance the experience for every player.' The AMX 67 Imbattable is then seen moving through a grassy field, firing its cannon amidst smoke and explosions, as the narrator explains the first improvement: 'stock vehicle configurations have been significantly improved,' smoothing out 'dips in their combat potential' during 'tech tree branch research.' Briefly returning to the digital interface, the narrator clarifies that while 'the top configuration will remain the strongest,' vehicles will now 'feel more comfortable to play right out of the box.' A close-up of the Type 4 Chi-To tank in a dimly lit garage appears, its upgrade tree highlighting 'Type 4 Chi-To Production' tracks and their specifications, as the narrator announces the second change: 'the module system has been simplified,' with 'all radio modules apart from the top ones' removed, and 'suspension no longer affects load capacity,' eliminating 'restrictions on modules and equipment for stock configurations.' The video concludes with a close-up of a tank's long barrel and tracks moving through grass in the Tank Formation Field, with national flags fluttering, as the narrator confirms these changes apply to 'all researchable vehicles in the game.'
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with a sweeping aerial view of the Tank Formation Field, where hundreds of tanks are arranged in precise rows across a vast grassy plain, each section marked by national flags including those of the USA, UK, China, Japan, Germany, France, and others, set against a backdrop of mountains and a distant castle. The scene transitions to a dark, digital interface displaying a collage of tank profiles and upgrade trees, labeled 'THE BIG REBALANCE', showcasing various tanks like the E 50 and Panhard EBR 90 with detailed stats and progression paths. The narrative then shifts to a dynamic combat sequence on a tropical beach, where a tank fires its cannon, splashes through shallow water, and maneuvers around rocky terrain with palm trees under a bright sky. This is followed by a close-up of the Type 4 Chi-To tank in a dimly lit garage, with its upgrade tree visible, highlighting the 'Type 4 Chi-To Production' tracks and their specifications such as traverse speed and suspension repair time. The video concludes with a return to the Tank Formation Field, now showing a close-up of a tank’s long barrel and tracks moving through grass, with national flags fluttering in the wind, reinforcing the global scale of the game’s rebalance.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a sweeping aerial view of the Tank Formation Field, where hundreds of tanks are arranged in precise rows across a vast grassy plain, marked by national flags, set against mountains and a distant castle. A male narrator, accompanied by heroic orchestral music, states that "targeted improvements have been made to hundreds of vehicles across multiple tiers." The scene transitions to intense combat sequences, showing camouflaged tanks maneuvering through a sunlit forested landscape, engaging in firefights with explosions and gunfire, as the narrator explains that "researchable light tanks have gained more hit points." A close-up follows, showing a dark-colored tank firing its cannon in a rocky, mountainous terrain, with the narrator adding that "a number of premium tanks have been improved," accompanied by the distinct sound of cannon fire. The action then shifts to a war-torn urban environment, where tanks battle amidst crumbling buildings and debris, with one enemy tank, the Cameron AX, being destroyed. The narrator specifies that "many underperforming Tier 10s have been buffed, including the Type 5 Heavy and much more," while the sounds of explosions and combat persist. The video then cuts to a desert battlefield, showing tanks advancing through sandy terrain with dust trails, as the narrator assures viewers that "all this will improve your experience as you progress through any tech tree branch." The narrative progresses to the Game Interface Screen, displaying detailed matchmaker lists for Allies and Enemies, with tank names like BZ-68, T-54, and IS-2-II, and a 'SEARCHING...' status that transitions to 'APPROVED'. The narrator emphasizes that "the big rebalance will improve the quality of battles, especially in tandem with the new matchmaker." The World of Tanks 2.0 Logo then appears, followed by a network of gameplay clips radiating from the logo, accompanied by a swell in the orchestral music and a subtle whoosh sound. This leads into a live battle on a grassy field with tanks from both sides engaging in combat, marked by red and green indicators, as the narrator states, "The matchmaker is essential for a comfortable gaming experience." The battle continues, then returns to the Game Interface Screen/Matchmaker as the narrator explains, "To make it smarter and more flexible, we've almost completely rewritten its core, its very foundation." The final scenes show tanks maneuvering on a dusty road in a desert environment, with the matchmaker interface still visible, indicating ongoing gameplay, while the narrator concludes that "battles now feature the most varied possible vehicle lineups, even when there are a lot of similar vehicles in the queue." The matchmaker interface remains visible as the narrator begins to explain, "Teams are more often built with a..." before the audio cuts off.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a cinematic sequence of tanks maneuvering through a rural landscape, featuring autumnal trees, wooden fences, and a windmill under a bright, clear sky, as a calm male voice explains matchmaker mechanics, stating that 'one tier difference' and 'vehicle rolls are taken into account.' The camera follows a tank from a third-person perspective along a dirt path, then transitions to a top-down view showing multiple tanks in formation with green and red icons, while the voice details how 'the matchmaker will put a Badger against a T110E3, not against a Grill 15.' The scene shifts to a split-screen view of two opposing tank formations circling a central structure, with the voice continuing to explain that 'teams are limited to three light tanks and five tank destroyers each.' The camera then focuses on individual tanks, including a close-up of a tank with a long barrel and distinctive blue-green camouflage, moving through a grassy field, as the speaker emphasizes that 'the matchmaker now adapts better,' reacting to player numbers and vehicle distribution to find 'the best compromise between reducing wait times and assembling the two most balanced teams.' The video transitions to a matchmaker interface displaying a list of allies and enemies with tank names and tiers, followed by a black screen with the text '2.0 NEW STORY MISSION'. This is succeeded by a detailed blueprint of a missile or projectile labeled 'ENL-7 Coil Assembly', with technical annotations and cross-sections. The final frames show a collage of black-and-white photographs pinned to a wall, depicting a coastal military installation with a watchtower and a circled target, with the word 'NORDSKAR' prominently displayed in green text, accompanied by a deep, resonant hum, as a new, ominous male voice declares, 'The world is on the brink of great changes,' adding that 'their source is a tiny island.'

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a dark, atmospheric shot of a rocket launch site, displaying 'NEW STORY MISSION' text, as a male voice dramatically states to "use all means at your disposal to stop the inevitable." The camera then moves through a circular tunnel, revealing a massive rocket being assembled on a launch pad, with the text updating to 'THIRD PERSONAL MISSIONS CAMPAIGN'. The male voice continues, explaining that "The secrets of Nordscar Island will lead you to Sector 3," as the scene transitions to a large, rusted metal door marked with a glowing white diamond-shaped symbol labeled 'Sector 3'. A distinct door opening sound is heard as the door opens to reveal a dimly lit, expansive industrial hangar. The voice introduces "The Third Personal Missions Campaign," describing it as "an all-new challenge for your skills that's drastically different from the two previous campaigns, with new conditions and worthy rewards." Inside the hangar, a red tank is suspended by a crane, surrounded by engineers and equipment, with the text '2.0 THIRD PERSONAL MISSIONS CAMPAIGN' remaining on screen. The camera pans across the hangar, showing various tanks and machinery, while the male voice elaborates that "The campaign consists of three operations, each of which is divided into three series of missions: Vanguard, Ambush, Assistance." The camera then focuses on a sleek, futuristic digital interface displaying a mission tree for 'Windhund', 'Dravec', and 'Black Rock' sectors, highlighting these mission types. The voice further explains that "Each series is completed in vehicles of corresponding roles" and that "No missions are restricted by vehicle type or nation, just three general groups by role." The camera zooms in on specific missions, such as 'Vanguard-6', which requires blocking 1,500 HP of damage while stationary or causing 2,500 HP of damage to enemy vehicles, with green checkmarks indicating completed tasks. The male voice concludes, assuring that "The mission conditions may not be easy, but they're all achievable," as the interface's glowing text and icons emphasize the structured progression of the campaign, all set against a backdrop of suspenseful, orchestral background music.

Dramatic, slightly eerie background music begins as on-screen text labels like 'THIRD PERSONAL MISSIONS CAMPAIGN' and '2.0 NEW MAP' appear. The narrator introduces this new environment, stating, "The island where the Sector 3 story begins isn't just the setting for a solo mission. It's also a full-fledged random battles map." He then names it, "Nordskar is a new battlefield that will transport you to a Norwegian island turned secret base." The visuals showcase a vast, diverse environment with lush green valleys, rocky cliffs, and a coastal shoreline under a cloudy sky, highlighting a rocket launch facility, an industrial complex by the sea, and a watchtower on a grassy island. The narrator continues, explaining that "The launchpad and design workshops create cover and key positions on two different flanks," adding that "The workshops are the arena for close-range brawls." The camera pans across the map, showing a split-screen comparison of the now-destroyed launch site and industrial zone, filled with smoke. A tank navigates the war-torn industrial area, and a high-altitude view reveals a watchtower under attack, erupting in flames, while the narrator concludes, "In the center of the map, you'll have plenty of space to pick up speed, then find places to stop and spot." The overall atmosphere is tense and immersive, blending natural beauty with the chaos of warfare, emphasizing the scale and variety of the new map.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a cinematic sequence showcasing the new map, Nordscar, in World of Tanks 2.0, accompanied by engine sounds. A dark-colored tank navigates rocky, mountainous terrain before transitioning to a wide aerial view of the vast, diverse battlefield. A narrator explains that the launchpad area offers "enough space for maneuverable combat and terrain with cover," with its "underground part also provides opportunities for flanking and sudden strikes." The environment features lush green valleys, rugged cliffs, a coastal shoreline, a rocket launch pad, and an industrial complex by the sea, all under a dynamic sky with mist and clouds. The camera pans across the map, highlighting key strategic points like a watchtower on a grassy island and a large rocket launch structure, with visible destruction and combat elements like explosions and smoke. The narrator encourages players to "Explore this new map and its unique atmosphere" and "Find your own ways to victory on Nordscar." The scene shifts to a close-up of a tank firing its cannon, followed by a dramatic explosion near the rocket pad. A transition sound then introduces the 'updated armor penetration indicator' feature, visually displaying a targeting reticle with a red outline highlighting enemy tank armor, distance, and penetration indicators. The narrator details that "Pressing the Y button lets you highlight the armor, not only at the aiming point but also of the surrounding area," activating "with a slight delay after you stop and start aiming," and working "only in sniper mode, within a small radius inside the aiming circle, and at a distance of up to 300 meters." On-screen text notes that additional features are disabled by default. The sequence continues with a tank moving through a sunlit field, firing at an enemy tank, and culminating in a fiery explosion. The final frames showcase the 'New Sounds' feature, with a camouflaged tank moving through a rural landscape, emitting engine smoke, and engaging in combat, as the narrator states, "Sound is another important part of the game," emphasizing the game's enhanced audio-visual experience.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a camouflaged tank moving through a rural landscape, emitting smoke from its engine, as the on-screen text 'NEW SOUNDS' appears. A male narrator explains that this 'deepens immersion and provides additional information about what's happening around you,' emphasizing that they've 'diversified our audio using real tank sounds.' As the scene transitions to an aerial view of a modern tank on a dirt road and a tan tank '04087' advances through a dusty desert, the narrator highlights 'new engine and track effects,' making vehicles 'sound different and more importantly, realistic.' He confirms that 'all the audio was recorded using real combat vehicles at the Tank Museum in Bovington,' with realistic tank engine and track sounds, distant gunfire, and explosions heard throughout. The action intensifies with a close-up of a tank's cannon firing, causing a large explosion, followed by a green tank on a paved road and another in a muddy industrial area. The narrator adds that 'tank collisions now sound richer and more believable,' accompanied by a metallic crash sound. A massive explosion engulfs the screen in flames and smoke, and the narrator explains that 'destruction sounds vary depending on object size and materials,' as various explosion and debris sounds fill the air. The video then transitions to a tank in a war-torn urban environment, a cinematic shot of a tank in a forested area, and a tank on a bridge overlooking a European city. Finally, a tank moves through a rocky, mountainous area towards a reinforced bunker, culminating in an explosion near its entrance. The on-screen text '2.0 RANDOM EVENTS' appears, and the narrator announces, 'Update 2.0 brings random events to four more maps,' specifically mentioning, 'The Yards now has a bunker with a passage,' just before an explosion and distant aircraft sound. The diverse environments, from rural to urban and mountainous, are enhanced by dynamic lighting and atmospheric effects, all underscored by the realistic combat audio.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a chaotic, dust-filled explosion in an industrial setting, accompanied by the rumble of tanks, intense combat sound effects, and a dramatic, driving musical score, as a voiceover states the game "opens its doors to everyone." A tank then moves along train tracks amidst cargo cars, suggesting a battlefield environment, while the voiceover explains that the "Anscregion features periodically passing trains that create dynamic cover for moving forward or changing position." The camouflaged and battle-worn tank, visually identified as the Dravec, navigates through a war-torn landscape with crumbling stone structures and fiery explosions, indicating intense combat. The voiceover continues, describing game map features like "Redshire," where a downed airship's wreckage provides cover, and the "southern part of Paris," where bridge debris creates new tactical opportunities, all while explosions and tank fire punctuate the scene. The scene shifts to a cinematic view of a cityscape with the Eiffel Tower visible, followed by a massive explosion in a European-style urban environment, with debris flying and buildings collapsing, further emphasized by the voiceover encouraging players to "close in and crush them armor." Tanks are shown maneuvering through rubble and destroyed streets, engaging in close-quarters combat, with continuous sounds of battle. The sequence culminates in a victory screen displaying 'VICTORY' with detailed player and enemy lists, scores, and rewards, including 2,919 stars and 7 kills. During this, the voiceover transitions to discussing game features like "Tier 11," "rebalanced matchmaking," and an "improved interface." The final frames show a digital interface with mission trees for 'Windhund', 'Dravec', and 'Black Rock' sectors, highlighting completed objectives with green checkmarks, and a 'Daily and Weekly missions' label, indicating progression in a structured campaign, as the voiceover specifically mentions "Personal missions" and these sector names. The overall atmosphere is intense and dynamic, with rapid transitions between combat, destruction, and strategic interface elements, all under a dramatic, war-torn sky, underscored by the persistent, driving musical score and informative voiceover.

entity_library.Game Interface Screen: A detailed UI showing various tank profiles and upgrade trees, including the E 50 and Panhard EBR 90, with nodes for characteristics, modules, and research costs, displayed in a dark, digital collage format, which transitions to the 'Matchmaker Interface' as the narrator introduces the 'new matchmaker' and discusses its rebalance.
entity_library.New Garage Environment: A spacious, modern garage with high ceilings, glass-walled offices, multiple tanks, a motorcycle, and industrial equipment, illuminated by natural light from large windows, serving as the backdrop where 'all vehicle information is now displayed in a new window'.
entity_library.Vintage Motorcycle: A black, classic motorcycle with a round headlight, chrome details, and a leather seat, parked in a dimly lit workshop with numbered crates and tools around it.
entity_library.Concept 5 Tank: A tan-colored wheeled tank with a long barrel, marked '82 SA 01', displayed prominently in the garage and selected in the game interface.
entity_library.My Vehicles Menu: A grid-based interface showing a collection of tanks organized by nation, with icons for each vehicle and star ratings, where 'familiar filters still work' and the right side of the screen displays 'all the latest events, in-game activities, Battle Pass, daily missions'.
entity_library.New Playlist Interface: A dark screen with a dashed border for adding vehicles, displaying 'Add vehicles' and instructions to press '+' or drag tanks, with options to 'Save playlist' or 'Exit', where users can create 'custom vehicle lists' similar to 'music playlists for different moods'.
entity_library.TVP VTU: A tan-colored Czechoslovakian support medium tank with a long barrel, displayed in the garage with detailed stats and armor visualization, including 280mm turret armor and 395mm effective armor at 45°, shown in a 3D model with color-coded armor layers, appearing as the voiceover declares, 'All this is our new era,' against a backdrop of building orchestral music.
entity_library.E 100: A German assault heavy tank shown in 3D armor inspection mode, with color-coded armor layers and detailed stats, including 280mm turret armor and 395mm effective armor at 45°, used for comparison within the 'new armor tab' that helps users 'learn how to block damage effectively and where to aim to penetrate your opponent's armor' through a 'detailed breakdown of the vehicle's armor'.
entity_library.Tech Tree: A detailed progression map showing German tanks from Tier I to Tier XI, including the E 100, Maus, and Leopard 120, with costs and tier indicators, displayed in the game interface, which the narrator describes as containing 'seven heavy tanks, five medium tanks, three tank destroyers, and one light tank' across 'existing tech tree branches' with 'diverse play styles' and 'unique mechanics' for each 'Tier 11 vehicle'.
entity_library.Victory Screen: A post-battle interface displaying 'VICTORY' with player and enemy lists, scores, and rewards, including 2,919 stars and 7 kills. The audio during this segment discusses game features like 'Tier 11,' 'rebalanced matchmaking,' and an 'improved interface.'
entity_library.Cinematic 'X' Structure: A massive, metallic 'X' structure, now shown as 'XI' to denote Tier XI, superimposed over a battlefield with tanks, appearing as the voiceover declares, 'All this is our new era,' and the orchestral music continues to build.
entity_library.Tank in Grass Field: A dark-colored tank moving through a grassy field, firing its cannon, with smoke and explosions visible, set against a mountainous backdrop, shown in a cinematic sequence. The audio describes facing 'fierce resistance from new Tier 11 tanks' and the ability to 'capture the newest vehicles,' utilize 'three different tanks' to adapt tactics, employ 'new abilities,' and 'call in air support,' all accompanied by intense tank firing, engine, and explosion sound effects.
entity_library.War-Torn Urban Environment: A destroyed cityscape with crumbling buildings, rubble, and debris, where tanks engage in close-quarters combat, with explosions and smoke filling the air. The voiceover describes specific tactical features within such environments, mentioning 'Redshire' where a downed airship provides cover, and the 'southern part of Paris' where bridge debris creates new cover and ramps for aggressive play.
entity_library.Enemy Tank (T603): A tan-colored enemy tank with a long barrel, marked with 'T603', shown being targeted and destroyed by the player's tank in the urban environment.
entity_library.Enemy Tank (Cameron AX): A dark-colored enemy tank with a long barrel, marked with 'Cameron AX', shown being targeted and destroyed by the player's tank in the grassy landscape, implicitly one of the tanks involved in the combat described by the narrator, which includes 'underperforming Tier 10s' being buffed.
entity_library.AMX 67 Imbattable: A Tier XI French Support Heavy Tank with a distinctive blue-green camouflage, shown in combat and in the garage interface, confirmed by the voiceover as one of the "16 vehicles" benefiting from the "upgraded system exclusive to Tier 11" and having "unique playstyle" and "customization elements unique to Tier 11." Its cannon firing sound is heard during combat sequences.
entity_library.Elite Rewards: A screen displaying rewards for the AMX 67 Imbattable’s Elite Level, including a 'Destroyed Vehicles' stat tracker, a 'Lignes de Force' volumetric style, and a 'Beast' gun sleeve 3D attachment.
entity_library.Upgrade Tree Interface: A detailed, node-based progression map for the AMX 67 Imbattable, showing interconnected upgrade paths with costs in stars, including 'Reload Time - Firepower', 'Engine Power - Mobility', and other enhancements.
entity_library.Elite Rewards Screen: A dedicated interface displaying unlockable rewards for reaching Elite Level, including a stat tracker, a volumetric style, and a 3D gun attachment, visually confirmed and explicitly named by the voiceover as "customization elements unique to Tier 11," specifically "a stat tracker, volumetric 2D styles and gun sleeves." UI clicks are heard during its appearance.
entity_library.Type 4 Chi-To: A Japanese Tier VI Sniper Medium Tank shown in a dimly lit garage, with its upgrade tree visible, highlighting the 'Type 4 Chi-To Production' tracks and their specifications such as traverse speed and suspension repair time, serving as an example while the narrator explains the simplification of the module system.
entity_library.Type 4 Chi-To Production: A Tier VI track upgrade for the Type 4 Chi-To tank, displayed in the game interface with specifications including weight (10,500 kg), traverse speed (43 deg/s), suspension repair time (12.03 s), and costs (5,200 stars to research, 15,500 to purchase), used to illustrate the simplified module system where suspension no longer affects load capacity.
entity_library.Tank in Desert Environment: Tanks advancing through sandy terrain with dust trails, shown in a desert battlefield with sparse vegetation and rocky outcrops, representing the improved 'tech tree branch' experience mentioned by the narrator.
entity_library.Matchmaker Interface: A dark, digital screen displaying lists of Allies and Enemies with tank names, tiers, and status indicators like 'SEARCHING...' and 'APPROVED', with a central '2.0 MATCHMAKER' label, shown in a top-down battlefield view. A calm male voice extensively describes its advanced algorithms, explaining how it accounts for 'one tier difference' and 'vehicle rolls', specifically mentioning tank types like 'Badger', 'T110E3', and 'Grill 15', and detailing team limitations to 'three light tanks and five tank destroyers each', emphasizing its improved ability to 'adapt better' by reacting to player queues and vehicle distribution to create 'the two most balanced teams'.
entity_library.Battlefield Network Visualization: A central World of Tanks 2.0 Logo surrounded by radiating gameplay clips, connected by lines, symbolizing a network of battle scenarios, appearing with a swell of orchestral music and a subtle whoosh sound, representing the interconnectedness of the new game experience.
entity_library.New Story Mission Screen: A black screen with the text '2.0 NEW STORY MISSION' in white, transitioning to a detailed technical blueprint of a missile or projectile labeled 'ENL-7 Coil Assembly', with cross-sections and annotations like 'Y-56/V', 'AB7-0912', and 'COD: 77X-P', on aged, textured paper. An authoritative male voice identifies this as 'Operation Boiling Point,' a single-player story mission set in 'Nordskar,' focused on infiltrating a secret base, gathering intel on a new weapon, and stopping a satellite launch carrying data that 'could change everything.'
entity_library.Blueprint Diagram: A detailed technical drawing of a missile or projectile labeled 'ENL-7 Coil Assembly', showing side and cross-sectional views with annotations like 'Y-56/V', 'AB7-0912', and 'COD: 77X-P', on aged, textured paper.
entity_library.NORDSKAR Collage: A collage of black-and-white photographs pinned to a wall, depicting a coastal military installation with a watchtower and a circled target, with the word 'NORDSKAR' prominently displayed in green text. An authoritative male voice explicitly names this as 'Nordskar,' describing it as a 'brand new location' for 'Operation Boiling Point,' where the mission involves infiltrating a secret base, gathering intel on a new weapon, and preventing the launch of a data-carrying satellite.
entity_library.Explosion Sequence: A series of fiery, cinematic explosions occurring in various battlefields, culminating in a massive blast. The accompanying narration explains that destruction sounds vary depending on object size and materials, contributing to a richer and more believable auditory experience, with metallic crash sounds also noted for tank collisions.
entity_library.Third Personal Missions Campaign Interfa: A futuristic digital interface displaying mission trees for 'Windhund', 'Dravec', and 'Black Rock' sectors, with mission types 'Vanguard', 'Ambush', and 'Assistance', each containing numbered objectives and green checkmarks for completed tasks, and a 'Daily and Weekly missions' label. The voiceover explicitly mentions 'Personal missions' and the names 'Windhund,' 'Dravec,' and 'Black Rock' in relation to these sectors.
entity_library.Sector 3 Door: A large, rusted metal door with a glowing white diamond-shaped symbol and the text 'Sector 3', set within a stone-walled corridor with orange-lit sconces, which the narrator identifies as the starting point for 'the Sector 3 story'.
entity_library.Red Suspended Tank: A red tank with a long barrel, marked '15-464', suspended by a crane in the center of the industrial hangar, surrounded by engineers and equipment.
entity_library.Mission Tree Interface: A dark, digital interface with glowing white text and icons, displaying mission categories 'Vanguard', 'Ambush', and 'Assistance' for 'Windhund', 'Dravec', and 'Black Rock' sectors, with numbered objectives and completion indicators.
entity_library.New Map: A vast, diverse environment featuring lush green valleys, rocky cliffs, coastal shorelines, an industrial complex by the sea, and a watchtower on a grassy island, all shown through dynamic aerial and ground-level shots, with visible destruction and combat elements. The on-screen text '2.0 RANDOM EVENTS' and narration confirm that 'Update 2.0 brings random events to four more maps,' specifically mentioning 'The Yards now has a bunker with a passage,' enhancing the strategic depth of these new locations with varied destruction sounds.
entity_library.Rocket Launch Pad: A large, white rocket mounted on a metal launch structure in a mountainous valley, shown from a low-angle perspective with smoke and fire, accompanied by the initial build of an intense, orchestral score and the voiceover stating, 'And much more.'
entity_library.Industrial Complex by the Sea: A sprawling industrial facility on a coastal platform with storage tanks, buildings, and cranes, shown with smoke and fires, indicating damage from combat, serving as a key battlefield environment, all set to a dramatic orchestral score.
entity_library.Watchtower on Grass Island: A metal watchtower situated on a small, grassy island in a river, shown from an aerial perspective, with flames erupting from it during an attack.
entity_library.Coastal Shoreline: A rocky beach with dark sand and scattered boulders, meeting calm waters under a cloudy sky, shown in a wide, sweeping shot, emphasizing the map's natural beauty and strategic points.
entity_library.Armor Flashlight: A feature officially named the 'updated armor penetration indicator', visually represented by a targeting reticle with a red outline highlighting enemy tank armor, distance, and penetration indicators. The narrator explains that pressing the 'Y' button highlights armor at the aiming point and surrounding area, activating with a slight delay after aiming, and working only in sniper mode within a small radius inside the aiming circle, up to 300 meters. On-screen text notes 'ADDITIONAL ARMOR PENETRATION INDICATOR FEATURES ARE DISABLED BY DEFAULT. YOU CAN ENABLE THEM IN THE GAME SETTINGS.'.
entity_library.New Sounds: A feature highlighted with on-screen text, showcasing a camouflaged tank moving through a rural landscape, emitting engine smoke, and engaging in combat. The accompanying narration explains that the audio has been diversified using real tank sounds, with new engine and track effects making vehicles sound 'different and more importantly, realistic,' all recorded using real combat vehicles at the Tank Museum in Bovington, enhancing the game's overall immersion.
